page: Microsoft Azure D32as_v6 is a general-purpose server instance belonging to the Dav6 family. It is powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The instance features 32 dedicated virtual CPUs mapped to 16 physical cores with simultaneous multithreading (2.0 threads per core) running at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz. The CPU configuration includes 32 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable, allowing users to attach external storage volumes.

In benchmark evaluations, the D32as_v6 demonstrates strong single-core capabilities, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance ranking in the top tier (top 10%). Multi-core CPU performance via stress-ng falls within the average range. Cryptographic and memory operations are highly efficient, as evidenced by a strong rating in PassMark CPU encryption and PassMark memory tests. The instance also shows strong performance in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and Geekbench single-core and multi-core metrics. For lightweight machine learning workloads, it achieves top-tier text generation speeds of 329.08 tokens per second using a small 135M LLM model, though performance scales down to average levels for larger 7B and 70B models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D32as_v6 offers a balanced resource density, making it a viable option for workloads requiring dedicated CPU power without the premium cost of specialized memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated hardware. The absence of local storage means storage costs are decoupled, allowing for flexible external disk configurations. This server is optimized for general-purpose enterprise applications, web serving, software compilation, database operations, and moderate data processing tasks that benefit from high single-core frequency and a balanced memory-to-CPU ratio.
description: Microsoft Azure D32as_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Dav6 family, powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V74 processor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture at 3.7 GHz, it features 32 vCPUs (16 physical cores) and 128.0 GB of system memory, maintaining a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. The instance does not include local storage or GPUs but is Premium Storage capable. In benchmark testing, the D32as_v6 exhibits top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong multi-core capabilities, particularly in cryptographic execution, memory bandwidth, and text processing. It also delivers top-tier text generation speeds for small-scale language models. This balanced resource profile offers high qualitative cost efficiency for general-purpose enterprise workloads, including web serving, software compilation, database management, and moderate data processing tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D32as_v6 is a general-purpose server featuring 32 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and top-tier single-core performance, designed for enterprise applications and database workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D32as_v6 is a general-purpose server with 32 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs and 128 GB RAM, optimized for balanced enterprise workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors, top-tier single-core performance, and scalable external storage capabilities.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor at 3.7 GHz
128.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 1:4 resource ratio
Top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong cryptographic execution
Premium Storage capable with no bundled local disk or GPU resources
Optimized for web serving, database operations, and software compilation
